title: How to Make Moroccan Preserved Lemons with Sea Salt
description: Moroccan preserved lemons are incredibly easy to make using just two ingredients: lemons and sea salt. This traditional fermented recipe preserves the whole lemon and can be made in about 10 minutes of prep time, with a 4-week fermentation period resulting in tangy, umami-rich preserved lemons.

ingredients.item: g fresh lemons (about 4 to 5 small lemons)

steps: Wash all of the lemons and weigh out the salt in a bowl.
Cut the ends off the lemons, then starting at one end, cut each lemon into quarters, without cutting all the way through.
Over a large bowl, fill the crevices of the lemons with copious amounts of salt until all the salt is used up.
Start packing the whole lemons into a clean, large jar, wedging them in as tightly as possible, and lemon juice will be released in the process.
Once the jar is full, make sure all the lemons are submerged in lemon juice, adding juice from additional lemons if needed.
Place a lid on the jar and allow the lemons to cure at room temperature for 4 weeks, or up to a year for deeper flavor and darker color.
Once opened, store in the refrigerator indefinitely, rinsing off excess salt and brine before using the preserved lemons in recipes and discarding the pulp if desired.
